mainEntity.name: What does voice search and AEO optimization typically cost, and what drives the price up or down?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The biggest cost drivers for voice search and AEO are the volume of Q&A content you need, the complexity of schema implementation, and whether you use in-house resources or external experts. Expect a mix of upfront investment (content + markup) and ongoing optimization.

Unlike one-off SEO tweaks, AEO is more about continuously expanding and improving your answer library for conversational queries.

Key factors:
- Number of pages and questions to optimize
- Need for technical help with FAQPage, HowTo, LocalBusiness, and Speakable schema
- Whether you require custom integrations or analytics dashboards
- Ongoing monitoring and content refresh cadence

Practically, you can start lean by optimizing 20–30 priority questions on a few key pages, then scale to hundreds as ROI becomes clear. Budget both time and money for question research, content writing, and validation.

If your team is small, a focused AEO engagement or software can be more cost-effective than trying to build all capabilities internally.
acceptedAnswer.description: Costs depend on how many Q&A pages you’re optimizing, how complex your schema needs are, and whether you use internal staff or outside experts. You can start small with a focused set of 20–30 high-value questions, then scale investment as results and ROI become clear.
